title: chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. hirsutum
intro: Chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. hirsutum, also known as hairy chervil or woolly chervil, is a plant species in the parsley family (Apiaceae). It is a perennial herb with a long history of use in traditional medicine, particularly in European folk remedies. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which may help to alleviate digestive issues and reduce pain. Hairy chervil is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and can be used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
